017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.marshaller;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022import java.lang.reflect.*;
023import java.util.*;
024
025import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
027
028/**
029 * Convenience static methods for serializing objects with all supported Juneau marshalling formats.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * Designed for use with static imports to allow concise serialization code without explicitly
033 * referencing marshaller classes.
034 *
035 * <h5 class='figure'>Examples:</h5>
036 * <p class='bjava'>
037 *    <jk>import static</jk> org.apache.juneau.marshall.marshaller.MarshallUtils.*;
038 *
039 *    <jc>// Serialize — char-based formats return String.</jc>
040 *    String <jv>a</jv> = json(<jv>myBean</jv>);
041 *    String <jv>b</jv> = json5(<jv>myBean</jv>);
042 *    String <jv>c</jv> = xml(<jv>myBean</jv>);
043 *    String <jv>d</jv> = html(<jv>myBean</jv>);
044 *    String <jv>e</jv> = yaml(<jv>myBean</jv>);
045 *
046 *    <jc>// Serialize — write to a stream.</jc>
047 *    json(<jv>myBean</jv>, myWriter);
048 *
049 *    <jc>// Serialize — binary formats return byte[].</jc>
050 *    <jk>byte</jk>[] <jv>f</jv> = msgPack(<jv>myBean</jv>);
051 *    <jk>byte</jk>[] <jv>g</jv> = cbor(<jv>myBean</jv>);
052 *
053 *    <jc>// Parse — same method names, overloaded by input type.</jc>
054 *    MyBean <jv>b1</jv> = json(<jv>jsonString</jv>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
055 *    MyBean <jv>b2</jv> = xml(<jv>xmlString</jv>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
056 *    MyBean <jv>b3</jv> = msgPack(<jv>bytes</jv>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
057 *
058 *    <jc>// Parse — parameterized types.</jc>
059 *    List&lt;MyBean&gt; <jv>list</jv> = json(<jv>jsonString</jv>, List.<jk>class</jk>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
060 * </p>
061 *
062 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
063 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Marshallers">Marshallers</a>
064 * </ul>
065 */
066public final class MarshallUtils {
067
068   private MarshallUtils() {}
069
070   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
071   // Char-based formats (return String)
072   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
073
074   /**
075    * Serializes a Java object to a JSON string.
076    *
077    * <p>
078    * A shortcut for calling <c>Json.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>object</jv>)</c>.
079    *
080    * @param object The object to serialize.
081    * @return The serialized object.
082    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
083    */
084   public static String json(Object object) throws SerializeException {
085      return Json.of(object);
086   }
087
088   /**
089    * Serializes a Java object to the specified JSON output.
090    *
091    * @param object The object to serialize.
092    * @param output The output object (Writer, OutputStream, File, or StringBuilder).
093    * @return The output object.
094    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
095    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
096    */
097   public static Object json(Object object, Object output) throws SerializeException, IOException {
098      Json.DEFAULT.write(object, output); return output;
099   }
100
101   /**
102    * Parses a JSON input to the specified Java type.
103    *
104    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
105    * @param input The input (Reader, CharSequence, InputStream, byte[], or File).
106    * @param type The object type to create.
107    * @return The parsed object.
108    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
109    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
110    */
111   public static <T> T json(Object input, Class<T> type) throws ParseException, IOException {
112      return Json.DEFAULT.read(input, type);
113   }
114
115   /**
116    * Parses a JSON input to the specified parameterized Java type.
117    *
118    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
119    * @param input The input (Reader, CharSequence, InputStream, byte[], or File).
120    * @param type The object type to create.
121    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
122    * @return The parsed object.
123    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
124    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
125    */
126   public static <T> T json(Object input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException, IOException {
127      return Json.DEFAULT.read(input, type, args);
128   }
129
130   /**
131    * Parses a JSON string to the specified Java type.
132    *
133    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
134    * @param o The JSON input string.
135    * @param c The object type to create.
136    * @return The parsed object.
137    */
138   public static <T> T json(String o, Class<T> c) {
139      return safe(() -> Json.to(o, c));
140   }
141
142   /**
143    * Parses a JSON string to the specified parameterized Java type.
144    *
145    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
146    * @param input The JSON input string.
147    * @param type The object type to create.
148    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
149    * @return The parsed object.
150    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
151    */
152   public static <T> T json(String input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException {
153      return Json.to(input, type, args);
154   }
155
156   /**
157    * Serializes a Java object to a JSON5 string.
158    *
159    * <p>
160    * A shortcut for calling <c>Json5.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>object</jv>)</c>.
161    *
162    * @param object The object to serialize.
163    * @return The serialized object.
164    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
165    */
166   public static String json5(Object object) throws SerializeException {
167      return Json5.of(object);
168   }
169
170   /**
171    * Serializes a Java object to the specified JSON5 output.
172    *
173    * @param object The object to serialize.
174    * @param output The output object (Writer, OutputStream, File, or StringBuilder).
175    * @return The output object.
176    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
177    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
178    */
179   public static Object json5(Object object, Object output) throws SerializeException, IOException {
180      Json5.DEFAULT.write(object, output); return output;
181   }
182
183   /**
184    * Parses a JSON5 input to the specified Java type.
185    *
186    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
187    * @param input The input (Reader, CharSequence, InputStream, byte[], or File).
188    * @param type The object type to create.
189    * @return The parsed object.
190    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
191    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
192    */
193   public static <T> T json5(Object input, Class<T> type) throws ParseException, IOException {
194      return Json5.DEFAULT.read(input, type);
195   }
196
197   /**
198    * Parses a JSON5 input to the specified parameterized Java type.
199    *
200    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
201    * @param input The input (Reader, CharSequence, InputStream, byte[], or File).
202    * @param type The object type to create.
203    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
204    * @return The parsed object.
205    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
206    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
207    */
208   public static <T> T json5(Object input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException, IOException {
209      return Json5.DEFAULT.read(input, type, args);
210   }
211
212   /**
213    * Parses a JSON5 string to the specified Java type.
214    *
215    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
216    * @param o The JSON5 input string.
217    * @param c The object type to create.
218    * @return The parsed object.
219    */
220   public static <T> T json5(String o, Class<T> c) {
221      return safe(() -> Json5.to(o, c));
222   }
223
224   /**
225    * Parses a JSON5 string to the specified parameterized Java type.
226    *
227    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
228    * @param input The JSON5 input string.
229    * @param type The object type to create.
230    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
231    * @return The parsed object.
232    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
233    */
234   public static <T> T json5(String input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException {
235      return Json5.to(input, type, args);
236   }
237
238   /**
239    * Serializes a Java object to a JSON Lines string.
240    *
241    * <p>
242    * A shortcut for calling <c>Jsonl.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>object</jv>)</c>.
243    *
244    * @param object The object to serialize.
245    * @return The serialized object.
246    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
247    */
248   public static String jsonl(Object object) throws SerializeException {
249      return Jsonl.of(object);
250   }
251
252   /**
253    * Serializes a Java object to the specified JSON Lines output.
254    *
255    * @param object The object to serialize.
256    * @param output The output object (Writer, OutputStream, File, or StringBuilder).
257    * @return The output object.
258    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
259    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
260    */
261   public static Object jsonl(Object object, Object output) throws SerializeException, IOException {
262      Jsonl.DEFAULT.write(object, output); return output;
263   }
264
265   /**
266    * Parses a JSON Lines input to the specified Java type.
267    *
268    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
269    * @param input The input (Reader, CharSequence, InputStream, byte[], or File).
270    * @param type The object type to create.
271    * @return The parsed object.
272    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
273    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
274    */
275   public static <T> T jsonl(Object input, Class<T> type) throws ParseException, IOException {
276      return Jsonl.DEFAULT.read(input, type);
277   }
278
279   /**
280    * Parses a JSON Lines input to the specified parameterized Java type.
281    *
282    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
283    * @param input The input (Reader, CharSequence, InputStream, byte[], or File).
284    * @param type The object type to create.
285    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
286    * @return The parsed object.
287    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
288    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
289    */
290   public static <T> T jsonl(Object input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException, IOException {
291      return Jsonl.DEFAULT.read(input, type, args);
292   }
293
294   /**
295    * Parses a JSON Lines string to the specified Java type.
296    *
297    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
298    * @param o The JSON Lines input string.
299    * @param c The object type to create.
300    * @return The parsed object.
301    */
302   public static <T> T jsonl(String o, Class<T> c) {
303      return safe(() -> Jsonl.to(o, c));
304   }
305
306   /**
307    * Parses a JSON Lines string to the specified parameterized Java type.
308    *
309    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
310    * @param input The JSON Lines input string.
311    * @param type The object type to create.
312    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
313    * @return The parsed object.
314    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
315    */
316   public static <T> T jsonl(String input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException {
317      return Jsonl.to(input, type, args);
318   }
319
320   /**
321    * Serializes a Java object to a canonical JSON string (RFC 8785).
322    *
323    * <p>
324    * A shortcut for calling <c>Jcs.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>object</jv>)</c>.
325    *
326    * @param object The object to serialize.
327    * @return The serialized object.
328    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
329    */
330   public static String jcs(Object object) throws SerializeException {
331      return Jcs.of(object);
332   }
333
334   /**
335    * Serializes a Java object to the specified canonical JSON output.
336    *
337    * @param object The object to serialize.
338    * @param output The output object (Writer, OutputStream, File, or StringBuilder).
339    * @return The output object.
340    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
341    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
342    */
343   public static Object jcs(Object object, Object output) throws SerializeException, IOException {
344      Jcs.DEFAULT.write(object, output); return output;
345   }
346
347   /**
348    * Parses a canonical JSON input to the specified Java type.
349    *
350    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
351    * @param input The input (Reader, CharSequence, InputStream, byte[], or File).
352    * @param type The object type to create.
353    * @return The parsed object.
354    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
355    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
356    */
357   public static <T> T jcs(Object input, Class<T> type) throws ParseException, IOException {
358      return Jcs.DEFAULT.read(input, type);
359   }
360
361   /**
362    * Parses a canonical JSON input to the specified parameterized Java type.
363    *
364    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
365    * @param input The input (Reader, CharSequence, InputStream, byte[], or File).
366    * @param type The object type to create.
367    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
368    * @return The parsed object.
369    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
370    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
371    */
372   public static <T> T jcs(Object input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException, IOException {
373      return Jcs.DEFAULT.read(input, type, args);
374   }
375
376   /**
377    * Parses a canonical JSON string (RFC 8785) to the specified Java type.
378    *
379    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
380    * @param o The JCS input string.
381    * @param c The object type to create.
382    * @return The parsed object.
383    */
384   public static <T> T jcs(String o, Class<T> c) {
385      return safe(() -> Jcs.to(o, c));
386   }
387
388   /**
389    * Parses a canonical JSON string (RFC 8785) to the specified parameterized Java type.
390    *
391    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
392    * @param input The JCS input string.
393    * @param type The object type to create.
394    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
395    * @return The parsed object.
396    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
397    */
398   public static <T> T jcs(String input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException {
399      return Jcs.to(input, type, args);
400   }
401
402   /**
403    * Serializes a Java object to an HJSON string.
404    *
405    * <p>
406    * A shortcut for calling <c>Hjson.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>object</jv>)</c>.
407    *
408    * @param object The object to serialize.
409    * @return The serialized object.
410    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
411    */
412   public static String hjson(Object object) throws SerializeException {
413      return Hjson.of(object);
414   }
415
416   /**
417    * Serializes a Java object to the specified HJSON output.
418    *
419    * @param object The object to serialize.
420    * @param output The output object (Writer, OutputStream, File, or StringBuilder).
421    * @return The output object.
422    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
423    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
424    */
425   public static Object hjson(Object object, Object output) throws SerializeException, IOException {
426      Hjson.DEFAULT.write(object, output); return output;
427   }
428
429   /**
430    * Parses an HJSON input to the specified Java type.
431    *
432    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
433    * @param input The input (Reader, CharSequence, InputStream, byte[], or File).
434    * @param type The object type to create.
435    * @return The parsed object.
436    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
437    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
438    */
439   public static <T> T hjson(Object input, Class<T> type) throws ParseException, IOException {
440      return Hjson.DEFAULT.read(input, type);
441   }
442
443   /**
444    * Parses an HJSON input to the specified parameterized Java type.
445    *
446    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
447    * @param input The input (Reader, CharSequence, InputStream, byte[], or File).
448    * @param type The object type to create.
449    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
450    * @return The parsed object.
451    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
452    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
453    */
454   public static <T> T hjson(Object input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException, IOException {
455      return Hjson.DEFAULT.read(input, type, args);
456   }
457
458   /**
459    * Parses an HJSON string to the specified Java type.
460    *
461    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
462    * @param o The HJSON input string.
463    * @param c The object type to create.
464    * @return The parsed object.
465    */
466   public static <T> T hjson(String o, Class<T> c) {
467      return safe(() -> Hjson.to(o, c));
468   }
469
470   /**
471    * Parses an HJSON string to the specified parameterized Java type.
472    *
473    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
474    * @param input The HJSON input string.
475    * @param type The object type to create.
476    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
477    * @return The parsed object.
478    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
479    */
480   public static <T> T hjson(String input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException {
481      return Hjson.to(input, type, args);
482   }
483
484   /**
485    * Serializes a Java object to an XML string.
486    *
487    * <p>
488    * A shortcut for calling <c>Xml.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>object</jv>)</c>.
489    *
490    * @param object The object to serialize.
491    * @return The serialized object.
492    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
493    */
494   public static String xml(Object object) throws SerializeException {
495      return Xml.of(object);
496   }
497
498   /**
499    * Serializes a Java object to the specified XML output.
500    *
501    * @param object The object to serialize.
502    * @param output The output object (Writer, OutputStream, File, or StringBuilder).
503    * @return The output object.
504    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
505    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
506    */
507   public static Object xml(Object object, Object output) throws SerializeException, IOException {
508      Xml.DEFAULT.write(object, output); return output;
509   }
510
511   /**
512    * Parses an XML input to the specified Java type.
513    *
514    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
515    * @param input The input (Reader, CharSequence, InputStream, byte[], or File).
516    * @param type The object type to create.
517    * @return The parsed object.
518    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
519    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
520    */
521   public static <T> T xml(Object input, Class<T> type) throws ParseException, IOException {
522      return Xml.DEFAULT.read(input, type);
523   }
524
525   /**
526    * Parses an XML input to the specified parameterized Java type.
527    *
528    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
529    * @param input The input (Reader, CharSequence, InputStream, byte[], or File).
530    * @param type The object type to create.
531    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
532    * @return The parsed object.
533    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
534    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
535    */
536   public static <T> T xml(Object input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException, IOException {
537      return Xml.DEFAULT.read(input, type, args);
538   }
539
540   /**
541    * Parses an XML string to the specified Java type.
542    *
543    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
544    * @param o The XML input string.
545    * @param c The object type to create.
546    * @return The parsed object.
547    */
548   public static <T> T xml(String o, Class<T> c) {
549      return safe(() -> Xml.to(o, c));
550   }
551
552   /**
553    * Parses an XML string to the specified parameterized Java type.
554    *
555    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
556    * @param input The XML input string.
557    * @param type The object type to create.
558    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
559    * @return The parsed object.
560    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
561    */
562   public static <T> T xml(String input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException {
563      return Xml.to(input, type, args);
564   }
565
566   /**
567    * Serializes a Java object to an HTML string.
568    *
569    * <p>
570    * A shortcut for calling <c>Html.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>object</jv>)</c>.
571    *
572    * @param object The object to serialize.
573    * @return The serialized object.
574    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
575    */
576   public static String html(Object object) throws SerializeException {
577      return Html.of(object);
578   }
579
580   /**
581    * Serializes a Java object to the specified HTML output.
582    *
583    * @param object The object to serialize.
584    * @param output The output object (Writer, OutputStream, File, or StringBuilder).
585    * @return The output object.
586    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
587    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
588    */
589   public static Object html(Object object, Object output) throws SerializeException, IOException {
590      Html.DEFAULT.write(object, output); return output;
591   }
592
593   /**
594    * Parses an HTML input to the specified Java type.
595    *
596    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
597    * @param input The input (Reader, CharSequence, InputStream, byte[], or File).
598    * @param type The object type to create.
599    * @return The parsed object.
600    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
601    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
602    */
603   public static <T> T html(Object input, Class<T> type) throws ParseException, IOException {
604      return Html.DEFAULT.read(input, type);
605   }
606
607   /**
608    * Parses an HTML input to the specified parameterized Java type.
609    *
610    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
611    * @param input The input (Reader, CharSequence, InputStream, byte[], or File).
612    * @param type The object type to create.
613    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
614    * @return The parsed object.
615    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
616    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
617    */
618   public static <T> T html(Object input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException, IOException {
619      return Html.DEFAULT.read(input, type, args);
620   }
621
622   /**
623    * Parses an HTML string to the specified Java type.
624    *
625    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
626    * @param o The HTML input string.
627    * @param c The object type to create.
628    * @return The parsed object.
629    */
630   public static <T> T html(String o, Class<T> c) {
631      return safe(() -> Html.to(o, c));
632   }
633
634   /**
635    * Parses an HTML string to the specified parameterized Java type.
636    *
637    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
638    * @param input The HTML input string.
639    * @param type The object type to create.
640    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
641    * @return The parsed object.
642    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
643    */
644   public static <T> T html(String input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException {
645      return Html.to(input, type, args);
646   }

1257    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1258    */
1259   public static <T> T markdownDoc(Object input, Class<T> type) throws ParseException, IOException {
1260      return MarkdownDoc.DEFAULT.read(input, type);
1261   }
1262
1263   /**
1264    * Parses a document-mode Markdown input to the specified parameterized Java type.
1265    *
1266    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1267    * @param input The input (Reader, CharSequence, InputStream, byte[], or File).
1268    * @param type The object type to create.
1269    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
1270    * @return The parsed object.
1271    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1272    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1273    */
1274   public static <T> T markdownDoc(Object input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException, IOException {
1275      return MarkdownDoc.DEFAULT.read(input, type, args);
1276   }
1277
1278   /**
1279    * Parses a document-mode Markdown string to the specified Java type.
1280    *
1281    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1282    * @param o The Markdown input string.
1283    * @param c The object type to create.
1284    * @return The parsed object.
1285    */
1286   public static <T> T markdownDoc(String o, Class<T> c) {
1287      return safe(() -> MarkdownDoc.to(o, c));
1288   }
1289
1290   /**
1291    * Parses a document-mode Markdown string to the specified parameterized Java type.
1292    *
1293    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1294    * @param input The Markdown input string.
1295    * @param type The object type to create.
1296    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
1297    * @return The parsed object.
1298    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1299    */
1300   public static <T> T markdownDoc(String input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException {
1301      return MarkdownDoc.to(input, type, args);
1302   }
1303
1304   /**
1305    * Serializes a Java object to an INI/properties string.
1306    *
1307    * <p>
1308    * A shortcut for calling <c>Ini.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>object</jv>)</c>.
1309    *
1310    * @param object The object to serialize.
1311    * @return The serialized object.
1312    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
1313    */
1314   public static String ini(Object object) throws SerializeException {
1315      return Ini.of(object);
1316   }
1317
1318   /**
1319    * Serializes a Java object to the specified INI/properties output.
1320    *
1321    * @param object The object to serialize.
1322    * @param output The output object (Writer, OutputStream, File, or StringBuilder).
1323    * @return The output object.
1324    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
1325    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1326    */
1327   public static Object ini(Object object, Object output) throws SerializeException, IOException {
1328      Ini.DEFAULT.write(object, output); return output;
1329   }
1330
1331   /**
1332    * Parses an INI/properties input to the specified Java type.
1333    *
1334    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1335    * @param input The input (Reader, CharSequence, InputStream, byte[], or File).
1336    * @param type The object type to create.
1337    * @return The parsed object.
1338    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1339    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1340    */
1341   public static <T> T ini(Object input, Class<T> type) throws ParseException, IOException {
1342      return Ini.DEFAULT.read(input, type);
1343   }
1344
1345   /**
1346    * Parses an INI/properties input to the specified parameterized Java type.
1347    *
1348    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1349    * @param input The input (Reader, CharSequence, InputStream, byte[], or File).
1350    * @param type The object type to create.
1351    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
1352    * @return The parsed object.
1353    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1354    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1355    */
1356   public static <T> T ini(Object input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException, IOException {
1357      return Ini.DEFAULT.read(input, type, args);
1358   }
1359
1360   /**
1361    * Parses an INI/properties string to the specified Java type.
1362    *
1363    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1364    * @param o The INI input string.
1365    * @param c The object type to create.
1366    * @return The parsed object.
1367    */
1368   public static <T> T ini(String o, Class<T> c) {
1369      return safe(() -> Ini.to(o, c));
1370   }
1371
1372   /**
1373    * Parses an INI/properties string to the specified parameterized Java type.
1374    *
1375    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1376    * @param input The INI input string.
1377    * @param type The object type to create.

1379    * @return The parsed object.
1380    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1381    */
1382   public static <T> T ini(String input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException {
1383      return Ini.to(input, type, args);
1384   }

1386   /**
1387    * Serializes a Java object to a TOML string.
1388    *
1389    * <p>
1390    * A shortcut for calling <c>Toml.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>object</jv>)</c>.

1393    * @return The serialized object.
1394    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
1395    */
1396   public static String toml(Object object) throws SerializeException {
1397      return Toml.of(object);
1398   }

1400   /**
1401    * Serializes a Java object to the specified TOML output.
1402    *
1403    * @param object The object to serialize.
1404    * @param output The output object (Writer, OutputStream, File, or StringBuilder).
1405    * @return The output object.
1406    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
1407    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1408    */
1409   public static Object toml(Object object, Object output) throws SerializeException, IOException {
1410      Toml.DEFAULT.write(object, output); return output;
1411   }
1412

1414    * Parses a TOML input to the specified Java type.
1415    *
1416    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.

1420    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1421    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1422    */
1423   public static <T> T toml(Object input, Class<T> type) throws ParseException, IOException {
1424      return Toml.DEFAULT.read(input, type);
1425   }
1426
1427   /**
1428    * Parses a TOML input to the specified parameterized Java type.
1429    *

1432    * @param type The object type to create.

1436    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1437    */
1438   public static <T> T toml(Object input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException, IOException {
1439      return Toml.DEFAULT.read(input, type, args);
1440   }
1441

1443    * Parses a TOML string to the specified Java type.

1448    * @return The parsed object.

1452   }
1453
1454   /**
1455    * Parses a TOML string to the specified parameterized Java type.
1456    *
1457    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.

1465      return Toml.to(input, type, args);
1466   }

1489    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.

1550   /**
1551    * Serializes a Java object to a Protocol Buffers text string.
1552    *
1553    * <p>
1554    * A shortcut for calling <c>Prototext.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>object</jv>)</c>.
1555    *
1556    * @param object The object to serialize.
1557    * @return The serialized object.
1558    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
1559    */
1560   public static String proto(Object object) throws SerializeException {
1561      return Prototext.of(object);
1562   }
1563
1564   /**
1565    * Serializes a Java object to the specified Protocol Buffers output.
1566    *
1567    * @param object The object to serialize.
1568    * @param output The output object (Writer, OutputStream, File, or StringBuilder).
1569    * @return The output object.
1570    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
1571    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1572    */
1573   public static Object proto(Object object, Object output) throws SerializeException, IOException {
1574      Prototext.DEFAULT.write(object, output); return output;
1575   }
1576
1577   /**
1578    * Parses a Protocol Buffers input to the specified Java type.
1579    *
1580    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1581    * @param input The input (Reader, CharSequence, InputStream, byte[], or File).
1582    * @param type The object type to create.
1583    * @return The parsed object.
1584    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1585    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1586    */
1587   public static <T> T proto(Object input, Class<T> type) throws ParseException, IOException {
1588      return Prototext.DEFAULT.read(input, type);
1589   }
1590
1591   /**
1592    * Parses a Protocol Buffers input to the specified parameterized Java type.
1593    *
1594    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1595    * @param input The input (Reader, CharSequence, InputStream, byte[], or File).
1596    * @param type The object type to create.
1597    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
1598    * @return The parsed object.
1599    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1600    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1601    */
1602   public static <T> T proto(Object input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException, IOException {
1603      return Prototext.DEFAULT.read(input, type, args);
1604   }
1605
1606   /**
1607    * Parses a Protocol Buffers text string to the specified Java type.
1608    *
1609    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1610    * @param o The Prototext input string.
1611    * @param c The object type to create.
1612    * @return The parsed object.
1613    */
1614   public static <T> T proto(String o, Class<T> c) {
1615      return safe(() -> Prototext.to(o, c));
1616   }
1617
1618   /**
1619    * Parses a Protocol Buffers text string to the specified parameterized Java type.
1620    *
1621    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1622    * @param input The Prototext input string.
1623    * @param type The object type to create.
1624    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
1625    * @return The parsed object.
1626    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1627    */
1628   public static <T> T proto(String input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException {
1629      return Prototext.to(input, type, args);
1630   }
1631
1632   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1633   // Binary formats (return byte[])
1634   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1635
1636   /**
1637    * Serializes a Java object to MessagePack bytes.
1638    *
1639    * <p>
1640    * A shortcut for calling <c>MsgPack.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>object</jv>)</c>.
1641    *
1642    * @param object The object to serialize.
1643    * @return The serialized object.
1644    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
1645    */
1646   public static byte[] msgPack(Object object) throws SerializeException {
1647      return MsgPack.of(object);
1648   }
1649
1650   /**
1651    * Serializes a Java object to the specified MessagePack output.
1652    *
1653    * @param object The object to serialize.
1654    * @param output The output object (OutputStream or File).
1655    * @return The output object.
1656    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
1657    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1658    */
1659   public static Object msgPack(Object object, Object output) throws SerializeException, IOException {
1660      MsgPack.DEFAULT.write(object, output); return output;
1661   }
1662
1663   /**
1664    * Parses a MessagePack input to the specified Java type.
1665    *
1666    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1667    * @param input The input (InputStream, byte[], or File).
1668    * @param type The object type to create.
1669    * @return The parsed object.
1670    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1671    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1672    */
1673   public static <T> T msgPack(Object input, Class<T> type) throws ParseException, IOException {
1674      return MsgPack.DEFAULT.read(input, type);
1675   }
1676
1677   /**
1678    * Parses a MessagePack input to the specified parameterized Java type.
1679    *
1680    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1681    * @param input The input (InputStream, byte[], or File).
1682    * @param type The object type to create.
1683    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
1684    * @return The parsed object.
1685    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1686    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1687    */
1688   public static <T> T msgPack(Object input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException, IOException {
1689      return MsgPack.DEFAULT.read(input, type, args);
1690   }
1691
1692   /**
1693    * Parses MessagePack bytes to the specified Java type.
1694    *
1695    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1696    * @param o The MessagePack input bytes.
1697    * @param c The object type to create.
1698    * @return The parsed object.
1699    */
1700   public static <T> T msgPack(byte[] o, Class<T> c) {
1701      return safe(() -> MsgPack.to(o, c));
1702   }
1703
1704   /**
1705    * Parses MessagePack bytes to the specified parameterized Java type.
1706    *
1707    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1708    * @param input The MessagePack input bytes.
1709    * @param type The object type to create.
1710    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
1711    * @return The parsed object.
1712    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1713    */
1714   public static <T> T msgPack(byte[] input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException {
1715      return MsgPack.to(input, type, args);
1716   }
1717
1718   /**
1719    * Serializes a Java object to CBOR bytes.

1721    * <p>
1722    * A shortcut for calling <c>Cbor.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>object</jv>)</c>.

1725    * @return The serialized object.
1726    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.

1729      return Cbor.of(object);

1739    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.

1742      Cbor.DEFAULT.write(object, output); return output;
1743   }
1744

1753    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.

1756      return Cbor.DEFAULT.read(input, type);

1758

1762    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.

1764    * @param type The object type to create.

1766    * @return The parsed object.
1767    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1768    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1769    */
1770   public static <T> T cbor(Object input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException, IOException {
1771      return Cbor.DEFAULT.read(input, type, args);
1772   }
1773
1774   /**
1775    * Parses CBOR bytes to the specified Java type.
1776    *
1777    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1778    * @param o The CBOR input bytes.
1779    * @param c The object type to create.
1780    * @return The parsed object.
1781    */
1782   public static <T> T cbor(byte[] o, Class<T> c) {
1783      return safe(() -> Cbor.to(o, c));
1784   }
1785
1786   /**
1787    * Parses CBOR bytes to the specified parameterized Java type.
1788    *
1789    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1790    * @param input The CBOR input bytes.
1791    * @param type The object type to create.
1792    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
1793    * @return The parsed object.
1794    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1795    */
1796   public static <T> T cbor(byte[] input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException {
1797      return Cbor.to(input, type, args);
1798   }

1800   /**

1803    * <p>

1808    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.

1811      return Bson.of(object);

1814   /**
1815    * Serializes a Java object to the specified BSON output.

1821    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.

1824      Bson.DEFAULT.write(object, output); return output;

1827   /**

1841   /**
1842    * Parses a BSON input to the specified parameterized Java type.

1848    * @return The parsed object.
1849    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1850    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.

1852   public static <T> T bson(Object input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException, IOException {
1853      return Bson.DEFAULT.read(input, type, args);
1854   }
1855
1856   /**
1857    * Parses BSON bytes to the specified Java type.
1858    *
1859    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1860    * @param o The BSON input bytes.
1861    * @param c The object type to create.
1862    * @return The parsed object.
1863    */
1864   public static <T> T bson(byte[] o, Class<T> c) {
1865      return safe(() -> Bson.to(o, c));
1866   }
1867
1868   /**
1869    * Parses BSON bytes to the specified parameterized Java type.
1870    *
1871    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1872    * @param input The BSON input bytes.
1873    * @param type The object type to create.
1874    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
1875    * @return The parsed object.
1876    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1877    */
1878   public static <T> T bson(byte[] input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException {
1879      return Bson.to(input, type, args);
1880   }
1881
1882   /**
1883    * Serializes a Java object to Apache Parquet bytes.
1884    *
1885    * <p>
1886    * A shortcut for calling <c>Parquet.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>object</jv>)</c>.
1887    *
1888    * <p class='warnbox'>
1889    *    <b>Note:</b> Parquet is collection-oriented. A single bean is automatically wrapped in a one-element list.
1890    * </p>
1891    *
1892    * @param object The object to serialize (Collection, array, or single bean).
1893    * @return The serialized object.
1894    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
1895    */
1896   public static byte[] parquet(Object object) throws SerializeException {
1897      return Parquet.of(object);
1898   }
1899
1900   /**
1901    * Serializes a Java object to the specified Parquet output.
1902    *
1903    * @param object The object to serialize.
1904    * @param output The output object (OutputStream or File).
1905    * @return The output object.
1906    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
1907    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1908    */
1909   public static Object parquet(Object object, Object output) throws SerializeException, IOException {
1910      Parquet.DEFAULT.write(object, output); return output;
1911   }
1912
1913   /**
1914    * Parses a Parquet input to a list of the specified Java type.
1915    *
1916    * <p class='warnbox'>
1917    *    <b>Note:</b> Parquet always returns a {@link List}; the parser never returns a bare instance.
1918    * </p>
1919    *
1920    * @param <T> The element type.
1921    * @param input The input (InputStream, byte[], or File).
1922    * @param type The bean class (parser returns {@code List<T>}).
1923    * @return The parsed list of beans.
1924    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1925    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1926    */
1927   @SuppressWarnings({
1928      "unchecked" // Parquet always returns List<T>; generic cast is safe by contract.
1929   })
1930   public static <T> List<T> parquet(Object input, Class<T> type) throws ParseException, IOException {
1931      return (List<T>) Parquet.DEFAULT.read(input, List.class, type);
1932   }
1933
1934   /**
1935    * Parses Apache Parquet bytes to a list of the specified Java type.
1936    *
1937    * <p class='warnbox'>
1938    *    <b>Note:</b> Parquet always returns a {@link List}; the parser never returns a bare instance.
1939    * </p>
1940    *
1941    * @param <T> The element type.
1942    * @param o The Parquet input bytes.
1943    * @param c The bean class (parser returns {@code List<T>}).
1944    * @return The parsed list of beans.
1945    */
1946   @SuppressWarnings({
1947      "unchecked" // Parquet always returns List<T>; generic cast is safe by contract.
1948   })
1949   public static <T> List<T> parquet(byte[] o, Class<T> c) {
1950      return (List<T>) safe(() -> Parquet.to(o, List.class, c));
1951   }
1952
1953   /**
1954    * Parses Apache Parquet bytes to a list of the specified element type.
1955    *
1956    * @param <T> The element type.
1957    * @param input The Parquet input bytes.
1958    * @param type The element type (parser returns {@code List<T>}).
1959    * @return The parsed list.
1960    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1961    */
1962   @SuppressWarnings({
1963      "unchecked" // Parquet always returns List<T>; generic cast is safe by contract.
1964   })
1965   public static <T> List<T> parquet(byte[] input, Type type) throws ParseException {
1966      return (List<T>) Parquet.to(input, List.class, type);
1967   }
1968
1969   /**
1970    * Parses Apache Parquet bytes to the specified parameterized Java type.
1971    *
1972    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
1973    * @param input The Parquet input bytes.
1974    * @param type The object type to create.
1975    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
1976    * @return The parsed object.
1977    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1978    */
1979   public static <T> T parquet(byte[] input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException {
1980      return Parquet.to(input, type, args);
1981   }
1982}
